steps.title: Identify the structural pivot
steps.content: The word atypical signals a shift from describing the exhibition to comparing it against the Nasher Museum's normal curatorial practice.

steps.title: Match the structure to the choices
steps.content: The text opens with an overview of the exhibition—its location, artists, and artistic disciplines—then closes by explaining what makes it unusual for the institution: its reliance on borrowed works instead of the permanent collection. A compares to other exhibitions broadly, B invents curator goals, and D claims the passage explains why disciplines were selected (it only lists them). That matches choice C — C is correct.
